Q.1) Computation of Total Income under the Head Salary in the hands of Mr. Kunal for the Assessment Year 2025-26 (115BAC)

| Particulars | ₹ | ₹ |
|-------------|---|---|
| 1) Basic Salary | | 7,22,000 |
| (60,000x10) + (61,000x2) | | |
| 2) Dearness Allowance | | 1,80,500 |
| 3) Bonus | | 60,000 |
| 4) Leave Encashment | | |
| (Note 1) | | |
| 5) Loan given at | | 2,00,000 |
| Lower Rate (Note 2) | | |
| 6) Rent free | | 92,000 |
| Accommodation | | |
| Accommodation (Note 3) | | |
| 7) Furniture provided | | 15,000 |
| free of cost | | |
| (Note 4) | | |
| 8) Electricity & | | 60,000 |
| Water bill paid | | |
| by the employer | | |
| (5000 x 12) | | |
| 9) Gift from colleague | | Nil |
| (Note 5) | | |
| Total Salary | | 13,29,500 |
| chargeable to tax | | |

---

Sure, here is the extracted handwritten text:

1. Notes

1) Since Assessee is opting for 115BAC entire Leave encashment become taxable.

2) Since it has opten for 115BAC entire loan become taxable.

3) In 115BAC entire Rent Free Accomodation provided by the employer are taxable.

4) The value of furniture & appliances shall be taken at 10% of Actual Cost Less Recovered which is (150,000 x 10%) = 15000.

5) Gift of mobile phone does not cover in the definition of Property And as per 56(2)(x) it is not chargeable to tax.

```
Losses to be carried forward

1) House Property Loss  31,000

Note
1) Loss incurred by the firm in which he a
partner is not eligible for Loss setoff since
share of profit is exempt.
2) House property Loss can be setoff maximum
upto ₹ 200,000 from Salary Remaining
has been carried forward.

Q.2)

=> b)

=> 1) As per section 194C if the contract value
exceed ₹ 100,000 in aggregate and ₹ 30,000 in
single payment then TDS shall be deducted
at 1%, for Individual and Hindu-undivided family
and 2% for others.
The tax deduction at source in this case
will be
= 300,000 x 1% = 3000
```

Sure, here is the extracted handwritten text:

```
Q.27 b7

(i) As per Section 194BB if Winning from & Horse
Races exceed ₹ 10,000 in a year then TDS
@ at the rate 30% will be applicable.
Therefore tax deducted in this case will be
= 150,000 x 30% = 45,000

(ii) As per Section 194LA in case of compulsary
compulsory acquisition if the compensation
exceed ₹ 250,000 in a year then only TDS
provision will be applicable. Since the consideration
does not exceed ₹ 200,000 then there will be
no tax deducted at such source.
```

```
Q.4

the following will be the consequence;
1) Loss from Business & Profession cannot be
carried forward
2) Capital Gain Loss cannot be carried forward

of the Act.
```

Q.27

=> a)

=> 1) As per the provision of section 10 when goods are supplied in Bill to ship to Model the place of supply will be the principal place of business of buyer. Therefore in the given case since the goods are delivered delivered to third party i.e. Shreyar of Shimoga Karnataka on request of by Parth of Pune, Maharashtra on request of the Bokul of Bareilly, Uttar Pradesh. Therefore the place of supply will be Bareilly Uttar Pradesh.

=> 2) In case of supply of services of organising the event the place of supply if the recipient is registered his principal place of business where he is registered. And they when are supplied to unregistered persons place of supply will be where the event actually held as per section 12. Therefore in the given since Damini Industries is Registered in Mumbai, Place of Supply will be Mumbai. No, the answer will remain same if even if the product launch party is organised at @ Dubai.

Q. 2)

=> 5 In case of supply of event organising services
the place of supply shall be the site place
where such event is actually performed since
the event is performed in Haryana
Therefore in the given case the place of
supply e & is Haryana in respect of music
Concert.
And in case of contract between Dhun Put
H Ltd and Dhanraj (p) Ltd the place of supply
shall be Ludhiana, Punjab, as Dhanraj (p) is
registered in Punjab.

Q.3)

⇒ a)

⇒ i) As per Section 31, - Time Limit to Issue Tax Invoice

The Tax Invoice shall be issued within 30 days from provision of service.

In this case the services are provided on 4th January so tax Invoice should have been issued on 4th February that is within 30 days.

Since in the given case Tax invoice has been issued on 10th February it is not within the time limit prescribed under Section 31 of the CGST Act, 2017

⇒ ii) The tax charged in respect of taxable supplies should be shown separately as per the provision of the Law.

So the contention of tax consultant of World Fashion & is valid as the amount of tax charged should be shown separately in the tax invoice.

Q.3)

=> b)

=> i) The following persons can opt for composition
scheme under section 10(1) & 10(2) whose
turnover in the preceeding financial year does
not exceed :
a) ₹ 75 Lakh (To Special category state)
b) ₹ 1.5 crore in remaining state.

Since M/s Balaji Electronics is registered in
Karnataka, it is eligible for higher threshold limit
that is ₹ 1.5 crore since their turnover does
not cross the these threshold limit, M/s Balaji
Electronics is eligible for composition scheme
under section 10(1) & 10(2) for the current
financial year.

=> ii) The firm can supply services along with goods
other than restaurant & catering services with
shall not exceed - Higher of
1) ₹ 500,000
or
2) 10% of the turnover i.e. 1200,000 ₹ (120 x 10%)

M/s Balaji Electronics can supply service upto
₹ 1200,000 and can be in composition
levy scheme u/s 10(1) & 10(2).
